questions.question: Which import statement is required to use the ArrayList class?
options: import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.lang.ArrayList;
import java.io.ArrayList;
import java.collections.ArrayList;
questions.answer: import java.util.ArrayList;

questions.question: What is the key difference between an array and an ArrayList?
options: An array can hold objects but an ArrayList cannot
An ArrayList is resizable while an array has a fixed size
An array uses generics but an ArrayList does not
An ArrayList can hold primitives but an array cannot
questions.answer: An ArrayList is resizable while an array has a fixed size

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid declaration of an ArrayList that holds integers?
options: ArrayList<int> nums = new ArrayList<>();
ArrayList<Integer> nums = new ArrayList<>();
ArrayList(Integer) nums = new ArrayList();
ArrayList[Integer] nums = new ArrayList[];
questions.answer: ArrayList<Integer> nums = new ArrayList<>();

questions.question: What does autoboxing mean in the context of ArrayList?
options: Automatically converting an ArrayList to an array
Automatically converting a primitive value to its corresponding wrapper object
Automatically sorting the elements in an ArrayList
Automatically resizing the ArrayList when it is full
questions.answer: Automatically converting a primitive value to its corresponding wrapper object

questions.question: Given: ArrayList<Integer> nums = new ArrayList<>(); nums.add(42); — what happens during the add call?
options: A compile error because 42 is a primitive
The int 42 is autoboxed to Integer.valueOf(42) and added
The int 42 is stored directly without conversion
A ClassCastException is thrown at runtime
questions.answer: The int 42 is autoboxed to Integer.valueOf(42) and added

questions.question: What does the method add(int index, E element) do on an ArrayList?
options: Replaces the element at the given index
Inserts the element at the given index, shifting subsequent elements to the right
Adds the element at the end and returns the index
Throws an exception if the index is not the last position
questions.answer: Inserts the element at the given index, shifting subsequent elements to the right

questions.question: Given: ArrayList<String> list = new ArrayList<>(); list.add("A"); list.add("B"); list.add("C"); — what does list.set(1, "X") do?
options: Inserts "X" at index 1, resulting in [A, X, B, C]
Replaces the element at index 1, resulting in [A, X, C]
Adds "X" at the end, resulting in [A, B, C, X]
Removes index 1 and adds "X" at the end
questions.answer: Replaces the element at index 1, resulting in [A, X, C]

questions.question: What does the size() method return for an ArrayList?
options: The maximum capacity of the ArrayList
The number of elements currently in the ArrayList
The number of null elements in the ArrayList
The index of the last element
questions.answer: The number of elements currently in the ArrayList

questions.question: What does the contains(Object o) method return?
options: The index of the object if found, -1 otherwise
The object itself if found, null otherwise
true if the ArrayList contains the specified element, false otherwise
The number of times the object appears in the ArrayList
questions.answer: true if the ArrayList contains the specified element, false otherwise

questions.question: What does the isEmpty() method return when called on a newly created ArrayList with no elements added?
options: false
true
null
questions.answer: true

questions.question: What is the result of calling clear() on an ArrayList?
options: It deletes the ArrayList object from memory
It sets the ArrayList reference to null
It removes all elements from the ArrayList, making it empty
It resets all elements to their default values
questions.answer: It removes all elements from the ArrayList, making it empty

questions.question: Given: ArrayList<Integer> nums = new ArrayList<>(); nums.add(10); nums.add(20); nums.add(30); — what does nums.remove(1) do?
options: Removes the element with value 1
Removes the element at index 1, which is 20
Removes the first element (10) from the list
Removes all elements equal to 1
questions.answer: Removes the element at index 1, which is 20

questions.question: Given an ArrayList<Integer> containing [10, 20, 30], how do you remove the element with value 10 (not index 10)?
options: nums.remove(10);
nums.removeValue(10);
nums.remove(Integer.valueOf(10));
nums.delete(10);
questions.answer: nums.remove(Integer.valueOf(10));

questions.question: Which wrapper class corresponds to the primitive type double?
options: Decimal
Float
Double
Number
questions.answer: Double

questions.question: What happens when you use a raw ArrayList without specifying a generic type?
options: The code will not compile
The ArrayList can hold any object type but loses type safety and may produce compiler warnings
The ArrayList automatically becomes ArrayList<Object> with full type safety
A runtime exception is thrown when adding elements
questions.answer: The ArrayList can hold any object type but loses type safety and may produce compiler warnings

questions.question: What happens when you attempt to remove elements from an ArrayList using an enhanced for loop while iterating?
options: The elements are removed successfully
A ConcurrentModificationException is thrown
The loop silently skips elements
An ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown
questions.answer: A ConcurrentModificationException is thrown

questions.question: Which approach correctly avoids index-shifting problems when removing elements from an ArrayList during iteration?
options: Iterating forwards with an enhanced for loop
Using a while loop that always increments the index
Iterating backwards with a standard for loop
Converting the ArrayList to an array first and iterating that
questions.answer: Iterating backwards with a standard for loop

questions.question: What is unboxing in Java?
options: Converting a String to a primitive type
Converting a wrapper object to its corresponding primitive value
Removing an element from an ArrayList
Extracting an array from an ArrayList
questions.answer: Converting a wrapper object to its corresponding primitive value

questions.question: Given: ArrayList<String> list = new ArrayList<>(); list.add("Hello"); list.add(null); list.add("World"); — what does list.get(1) return?
options: An empty string ""
The string "null"
null
It throws a NullPointerException
questions.answer: null

questions.question: Given: ArrayList<String> list = new ArrayList<>(); list.add("Hello"); list.add(null); — what happens when you call list.get(1).length()?
options: It returns 0
It returns 4
It throws a NullPointerException
It returns -1
questions.answer: It throws a NullPointerException

questions.question: Which method from java.util.Collections can be used to sort an ArrayList alphabetically?
options: Collections.order(list)
Collections.sort(list)
Collections.arrange(list)
list.sort()
questions.answer: Collections.sort(list)

questions.question: What is the correct enhanced for loop syntax to iterate over an ArrayList<String> called names?
options: for (names : String name) { }
for (String name : names) { }
for (name in names) { }
for (int i : names.size()) { }
questions.answer: for (String name : names) { }

questions.question: What does System.out.println(fruits) print when fruits is an ArrayList<String> containing "Apple", "Banana", "Cherry"?
options: [Apple, Banana, Cherry]
Apple Banana Cherry
ArrayList@7a81197d
{Apple, Banana, Cherry}
questions.answer: [Apple, Banana, Cherry]

questions.question: What does the indexOf(Object o) method return if the specified element is NOT found in the ArrayList?
options: null
It throws a NoSuchElementException

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the generic type syntax in ArrayList<String>?
options: It specifies that the ArrayList can hold any type including String
It restricts the ArrayList to hold only String objects, providing compile-time type safety
It causes the ArrayList to convert all elements to Strings automatically
It is optional syntax that has no effect on the behavior of the ArrayList
questions.answer: It restricts the ArrayList to hold only String objects, providing compile-time type safety
